pages.title: Cacao Ceremony Kit Everything You Need for Rituals
pages.metaDescription: Everything you need for a sacred cacao ceremony, from premium cacao to essential tools, carefully curated to guide you through a transformative experience.
h2s: Why 1 million cups of Ora Cacao are sipped each year...

Our pure cacaos are made from a single ingredient, organic cacao beans, that are minimally processed to create a user-friendly cacao disc for eating or making a cup of liquid cacao. While each pure cacao is the same ingredient, each has a different flavor and energetic profile that showcases cacao in all its uniqueness. Our cacao is organic certified and ethically traded with small farmers across a number of indigenous communities. (Glowing Guatemala is out of stock until further notice)

Enhanced cacaos are more than a “flavor”. Each one is an entirely unique experience of cacao as a medicine, bringing potent medicinal and energetic qualities. Through our custom process and global sourcing, we grind only the highest quality whole food ingredients into our 100% cacaos. Due to the vasodilating and cardiac stimulant properties of the cacao base, it acts as an optimal delivery mechanism for the superfoods we carefully select.

pages.h1: What You Need to Know About Cacao Genetics
h2s: When consumers of chocolate hear the word Criollo, what they are universally expecting is the ancient domestic Criollo that was grown by the Mayans and their forebears like the Olmec and Zaptoec. Yet ...

At Ora Cacao, we work with small cacao farmers in six different countries to source our ceremonial cacao. We purchase from thousands of small cacao farmers and run our own factory.

Use local region of origin descriptors for cacao classification

pages.mainSnippet: Growing cacao in diverse agroforestry systems, rather than simply on a monoculture plantation, is a fantastic method for protection and regeneration of tropical rainforest ecosystems. The cacao harvest alone can provide enough cash for farmers to support them with a good living while they operate in harmony with and protect the diverse life of the rainforest. Ceremonial cacao begins with the communities that we work with to source our cacao. Unlike the majority of chocolate consumed globally, we don’t source from centralized plantations. pages.title: How Purchasing Wet Cacao Improves Livelihoods
pages.h1: How Purchasing Wet Cacao Improves Livelihoods
h2s: Wet Cacao BREAKS THE POVERTY CYCLE
pages.mainSnippet: Cacao post harvest processing is instrumental to the social impact we have in the communities we work with, so it is worth taking a moment to read this. In ALL the regions we work in, until the last decade, smallholder cacao farmers had to perform significant post harvest processing of their own harvest before being able to sell it. Cacao farmers typically have two post harvest options: first, to ferment and dry their own cacao, or second, to "wash" the cacao. Neither is a great option, unfortunately.

pages.title: The Essential Science of Cacao Fermentation
pages.h1: The Essential Science of Cacao Fermentation
pages.mainSnippet: The best cacao beans in the world will not produce good chocolate if the post harvest processing is not completed properly! Post harvest processing consists of cacao fermentation, cacao drying, and finally hand sorting & blending. The process of fermenting cacao is very delicate and needs exacting attention to produce good results.

pages.title: Contextualizing the Impact of Colonization
h2s: Rainforest ecosystems became managed as a “Resource” rather than a “living system”
pages.mainSnippet: The cacao growing communities that we work in have all been substantially impacted by the colonization of the Americas beginning in the 1500s and continuing to this day. As responsible stewards of ceremonial cacao, understanding and sharing this history is a crucial part of our work. We’ll provide a framework here for colonization, what that looks like for cacao, what steps can be taken to decolonize, and why we believe ceremonial cacao is a crucial tool for restoring respect. To be colonized means to be disconnected and disintegrated from ancestry and land-based knowledge.

pages.metaDescription: Our single origin, organic, above fair trade cacao is sourced directly from small family farms along the Ucayali River in the heart of the Peruvian Amazon. It is grown using agroforestry, the most sustainable growing methods for the earth and cacao plants.  Agroforestry is a biodiverse ecosystem of varied plant and wil
pages.mainSnippet: Our single origin, organic, above fair trade cacao is sourced directly from small family farms along the Ucayali River in the heart of the Peruvian Amazon. It is grown using agroforestry, the most sustainable growing methods for the earth and cacao plants. Agroforestry is a biodiverse ecosystem of varied plant and wildlife species coexisting together, creating richer soil, healthier fauna, and a more balanced, vibrant environment for all living things. pages.mainSnippet:
Coconut Sugar
We sweeten our chocolate with unrefined, organic coconut sugar. Coconut sugar is a healthy, low-glycemic sweetener that offers sustained, balanced energy without the spike and crash of refined white sugar. It's rich in magnesium, calcium, potassium, zinc, iron, and supports blood sugar stability. It has a delicious sweet and caramelly taste, and creates a pristine flavor profile when paired with our Ucayali River Peruvian cacao. pages.mainSnippet: Featuring bars made from fine cocoa origins, the flavors and quality of our curated boxes is unmatched. This gift is fool-proof! Our Chocolate Club subscription is our most popular gift. Choose Dark & Vegan or our Adventure box and get 4 bars each month. Treat yourself or someone else to amazing chocolate!

pages.title: About Bar & Cocoa and Our Chocolate
pages.metaDescription: At Bar & Cocoa our craft chocolate is carefully selected best premium bean to bar chocolate. We carry craft chocolate makers from all over the world, all of them ethically sourced, and many of them award winning. Come shop our single origin bars or join our dark chocolate subscription.
pages.mainSnippet: Ethics is a key ingredient to the world’s best chocolate. Bar & Cocoa’s selection supports a supply chain that is not only more transparent than fair trade or big-brand chocolate but also tastier! The makers we feature source their cacao through direct trade. Therefore, local cacao farmers are paid better and makers can nurture higher standards for premium quality cacao and sustainable agroforestry. pages.title: Rewards Program – Bar & Cocoa
pages.mainSnippet: You can earn points with every purchase and completing various activities. Points can be redeemed for free products, discount codes and more. If you are logged in to your account, you'll see a section to refer your friends!

pages.title: Virtual Chocolate Tastings – Bar & Cocoa
pages.mainSnippet: Join a certified chocolate expert for a live & interactive fun virtual chocolate tasting. Experience an interactive chocolate tasting and learn about chocolate origins and tasting 5 full size chocolates bars. Featuring award winning chocolate makers from all over the world.
